intro.title: Crusader Castles and the Wilderness of Zin
intro.body: Lawrence walked Syria in 1909 photographing crusader castles for his Oxford thesis, then excavated at Carchemish under D.G. Hogarth from 1911. In 1914 he and Leonard Woolley surveyed the Wilderness of Zin in southern Palestine — ostensibly archaeology, in fact military intelligence for the coming war.

stops.name: Beirut
stops.note: Landfall by steamer, July 1909. Lawrence sets out on his castle walk with one bag and a Mauser pistol.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Ottoman Levantine port with paddle-steamer at quay, young Englishman in shirtsleeves stepping ashore.

stops.name: Krak des Chevaliers
stops.note: August 1909. Lawrence writes his thesis chapter inside the walls and photographs every face of the keep.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Vast crusader castle on a Syrian ridge, single figure with camera tripod on the inner ward.

stops.name: Carchemish
stops.note: Hittite site on the Euphrates; British Museum excavation 1911-1914 under Hogarth. Lawrence runs the dig house and learns Arabic.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Mud-brick dig house above a river bend, Turkish workmen carrying baskets of earth, neo-Hittite reliefs being lifted.

stops.name: Wilderness of Zin
stops.note: January-February 1914. Lawrence and Woolley survey the Negev under archaeological cover; the maps go to the War Office.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Desert wadi at dawn, two surveyors with theodolite, camel kneeling in foreground.

stops.name: Aqaba
stops.note: Lawrence reconnoitres the head of the Red Sea on the same trip. The town will fall to him three years later in July 1917.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Mud-brick coastal village under bare granite mountains, single dhow at anchor, surveyor sketching the bay.
